payload.name: Project analysis and code review
payload.description: Изучи проект kompoz (Node.js/Express web editor for Docker Compose), проведи полный анализ и код ревью. Важно чтобы ты создавал скиллы и память для этого проекта. Нужно: 1) Исследовать всю кодовую базу (backend, frontend, tests, конфиги), 2) Выявить проблемы безопасности, архитектурные недостатки, баги, код-smells, 3) Провести полный code review через review agent, 4) Создать project-specific skills в .kimchi/agents/ для будущей работы с этим проектом, 5) Создать память/документацию проекта.

goal.answer: Провести полный анализ и код-ревью проекта kompoz (Node.js/Express web editor for Docker Compose), выявить проблемы безопасности, архитектурные недостатки, баги и код-smells, а также создать project-specific скиллы и память для будущей работы.

payload.plain: Провести полный анализ и код-ревью проекта kompoz (Node.js/Express web editor for Docker Compose), выявить проблемы безопасности, архитектурные недостатки, баги и код-smells, а также создать project-specific скиллы и память для будущей работы.

criteria.answer:
Project overview document exists at `.kimchi/docs/project-overview.md` with full codebase map (structure, stack, entry points, dependencies)
Analysis report exists at `.kimchi/docs/analysis.md` with at least 10 classified issues (Critical/High/Medium/Low severity) and concrete suggested fixes for each
Independent code review report exists at `.kimchi/docs/review.md` with verdict (APPROVED/NEEDS_FIXES) and file:line references
Existing tests have been executed (`npm test`) and results documented
At least one project-specific skill exists under `.kimchi/agents/` with a valid `SKILL.md` following the agentskills.io spec
Project memory document exists at `.kimchi/docs/memory.md` (or `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`) documenting conventions, architectural decisions, and known issues

plain: Project overview document exists at `.kimchi/docs/project-overview.md` with full codebase map (structure, stack, entry points, dependencies)
Analysis report exists at `.kimchi/docs/analysis.md` with at least 10 classified issues (Critical/High/Medium/Low severity) and concrete suggested fixes for each
Independent code review report exists at `.kimchi/docs/review.md` with verdict (APPROVED/NEEDS_FIXES) and file:line references
Existing tests have been executed (`npm test`) and results documented
At least one project-specific skill exists under `.kimchi/agents/` with a valid `SKILL.md` following the agentskills.io spec
Project memory document exists at `.kimchi/docs/memory.md` (or `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`) documenting conventions, architectural decisions, and known issues

constraints.answer: Do not modify production code without explicit user request (explore + review only), Do not add new dependencies to the project, Store all artifacts in `.kimchi/docs/` or `.kimchi/agents/`, Use only existing tools and patterns found in the project

plain: Do not modify production code without explicit user request (explore + review only)
Do not add new dependencies to the project
Use only existing tools and patterns found in the project

phases.answer: Explore, Analysis, Code Review, Skills & Memory

phaseSnapshots.name: Explore
phaseSnapshots.goal: Understand full project structure, key modules, integration points, and potential risks
phaseSnapshots.description: Complete codebase exploration: server (Express routes, auth, utils), frontend (vanilla JS modules), tests (node:test), configuration (Docker, package.json, locales). Produce a project map.

steps.description: Read all remaining server and frontend files not yet examined
Read tests, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and locales
Write project-overview.md with full codebase map

phaseSnapshots.name: Analysis
phaseSnapshots.goal: Identify and document at least 10 specific issues with file:line references and remediation guidance
phaseSnapshots.description: Analyze discovered code for security flaws, architectural issues, bugs, performance, and maintainability. Classify findings by severity and provide suggested fixes.

steps.description: Analyze auth, path-traversal protection, input validation, and error handling
Analyze frontend for XSS, CSRF, unsafe DOM insertion, and event handling
Analyze test coverage and lint rule configuration
Write analysis.md with classified findings and suggested fixes

phaseSnapshots.name: Code Review
phaseSnapshots.goal: Obtain an independent quality assessment with a clear verdict
phaseSnapshots.description: Dispatch an independent reviewer agent to audit the codebase in a fresh context, verifying the analysis findings and catching additional issues.

steps.description: Run tests and record output for the reviewer
Dispatch reviewer agent with full project context and analysis findings
Verify reviewer report contains verdict and file:line references

phaseSnapshots.name: Skills & Memory
phaseSnapshots.goal: Create reusable artifacts that accelerate future work on the project
phaseSnapshots.description: Create project-specific agent skills and persistent memory documenting conventions, architecture, and known issues for future sessions.

steps.description: Create at least one project-specific skill under .kimchi/agents/
Create project memory document (CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md) with conventions and architectural decisions
Create memory.md with known issues and review outcomes

assumptions.answer: The project runs on Node.js 20 with Express 4, uses vanilla JS frontend (no framework), CodeMirror 5, D3 for map visualization; Target audience is Russian-speaking (README.ru.md, comments in Russian), but documentation/skills should be in English for reuse; Deployment is via Docker Compose; `COMPOSE_MOUNTS` defines working directories inside the container; Auth is optional cookie-based HMAC; disabling auth leaves the app completely open; User wants a comprehensive review covering security, architecture, bugs, and maintainability with suggested fixes

payload.summary: Completed full exploration of the kompoz codebase. Read all server routes, utilities, frontend modules, HTML entry points, tests, Docker configs, and locales. Produced `.kimchi/docs/project-overview.md` with full codebase map including directory structure, stack details, entry points, key module descriptions, auth mechanism, and environment configuration.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 2 completed. Combined backend, frontend, and test/lint audits into `.kimchi/docs/analysis.md` with 13 classified issues (2 Critical, 4 High, 5 Medium, 2 Low), each including concrete file:line references and suggested fixes. Also produced transient audit notes for backend, frontend, and test coverage.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 3 completed. Tests were executed (1 passed, 0 failed). An independent reviewer agent produced `.kimchi/docs/review.md` with verdict NEEDS_FIXES, a findings table with file:line references, additional independent findings, and an overall assessment.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 4 completed. Created `.kimchi/agents/kompoz-project/SKILL.md` (valid agentskills.io spec skill), `.kimchi/agents/CLAUDE.md` (project memory with conventions and architectural decisions), and `.kimchi/docs/memory.md` (known issues and review outcomes).

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.finalSummary:
Full analysis and code review of kompoz completed.

Deliverables:
- `.kimchi/docs/project-overview.md` — full codebase map, stack, entry points, dependencies
- `.kimchi/docs/analysis.md` — 13 classified security and quality issues (2 Critical, 4 High, 5 Medium, 2 Low) with concrete suggested fixes
- `.kimchi/docs/review.md` — independent review with NEEDS_FIXES verdict, file:line references, and additional findings
- `.kimchi/docs/test-output.txt` — npm test results (1 passed, 0 failed)
- `.kimchi/agents/kompoz-project/SKILL.md` — project-specific skill following agentskills.io spec
- `.kimchi/agents/CLAUDE.md` — project memory with conventions, architecture, and known issues
- `.kimchi/docs/memory.md` — review outcomes and known issues summary

No production code was modified.

grade.rationale: Multiple phases relied on proxy verification—Explore and Analysis F1 gates explicitly used file-existence checks and grep rather than concrete evidence, and the gate rationales cite generic methods without naming specific files, commands, or outputs. Although all deliverables were produced and npm test ran, the thin substantiation and lack of grounded artifact validation prevent an A.
